title: Buccopharyngeal morphology of tadpoles in Scanning Electron Microscope

steps: Wash quickly the specimens in tap water and dissect tadpoles following Wassersug (1976: p. 4-5) . Usually tadpoles are fixed and conserved in 10% formalin, buffered or not. I'm here assuming that your samples are already in formalin.
Fix the dissections in 4% Glutaraldehyde diluted in the fixing solution 02:30:00 Glutaraldehyde EM Grade 25% Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G5882-50ML

Post fix the dissections in 1% Osmium tetroxide solution. undetermined 02:30:00 Usually you'd have a 2% solution and you'd have to dilute it in Millonig's phosphate buffer solution until your dissections are fully covered. Safety check: this solution is highly toxic and volatile, so this procedure is better done under exhaust hoods. At the end of this step all your specimens would have to be entirely black. Osmium tetroxide solution 2% Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #75633
Dehydrate in an ascending series of acetones, starting at 30% undetermined 00:15:00
Transfer to another solution of acetone 95% undetermined 00:15:00
Transfer to another solution of acetone 95% undetermined 00:15:00
Transfer to another solution of acetone 100% undetermined 00:15:00

Now take your specimens to a Critical Point Dryer http://www.leica-microsystems.com/products/em-sample-prep/biological-specimens/room-temperature-techniques/drying/details/product/leica-em-cpd300/ Specimens are completely dried, rigid, and somewhat whitish
Mount the specimens on stubs and attach them with a double-sided tape https://us.vwr.com/store/catalog/product.jsp?catalog_number=100492-314

Take the specimens to the High Vaccum Coater and you're ready to analyze them. http://www.leica-microsystems.com/pt/produtos/preparacao-de-amostras-para-microscopia-eletronica/especimes-biologicos/tecnicas-em-temperatura-ambiente/revestimento/detalhes/product/leica-em-ace600/
creator: Diogo B. Provete
